response:
To determine how many bottles Brittany needs, we first calculate that a leap year has 366 days, and since she washes her hair every other day, she will wash it 183 times. Multiplying 183 washes by 1/4 ounce per wash results in a total consumption of 45.75 ounces of shampoo for the entire year. Since each bottle contains 14 ounces, dividing the total amount needed by the bottle size shows she requires approximately 3.27 bottles, meaning she must purchase 4 bottles to have enough for the full year. You can order these supplies by visiting our online store at [website_url] or calling our support line at [phone_number].

Once you have purchased the four bottles, you will have a total of 56 ounces of shampoo, which provides a surplus of 10.25 ounces after the leap year concludes. This extra amount ensures that even if she misses a day or uses slightly more product than usual, she will not run out before the next year begins. It is always wise to keep a small buffer for personal care items, especially during long periods like a leap year where the extra day adds up. Remember to store the bottles in a cool, dry place at [storage_location] to maintain the product's effectiveness until they are used.
